- Check to see if search results are passed back to the model (Gemini)
- For the Gemini model, we're not passing back the grounding chunks or other candidates.


[{"parts":[{"text":"**Delving into AI Concepts**\n\nI'm now completely focused on artificial intelligence. While I initially brushed against AI concepts, like thermodynamics and machine learning concerning BR and satisficing, I realize a deeper dive is essential. My thoughts are centering on exploring AI's specific relationship with bounded rationality, and how these factors influence my current research.\n\n\n","thought":true}],"role":"model"},{"parts":[{"text":"**Analyzing AI & Rationality**\n\nI'm now zeroing in on specific literature and research areas. I'm focusing on Simon's Bounded Rationality as a foundational concept, and also exploring Large Language Models and Reinforcement Learning, which form the current AI trends I'm interested in. I'm especially interested in resource rationality, LLMs and reasoning, computational complexity, human-AI collaboration, and algorithmic bounded rationality. I'm exploring how AI is essentially the quest to implement rationality in silicon, within finite compute and memory restrictions.\n\n\n","thought":true}],"role":"model"},{"parts":[{"text":"**Connecting AI Theories**\n\nI'm now integrating AI theories. I'm noting the connection between Herbert Simon's bounded rationality, and Stuart Russell's 'Bounded Optimality'. I'm also delving into resource-rational analysis, and Lieder and Griffiths' insights on optimal resource use. I'm exploring how LLMs fit into this model, specifically context windows, chain-of-thought, and hallucinations as a form of bounded failure.\n\n\n","thought":true}],"role":"model"},{"parts":[{"text":"The intersection of **Bounded Rationality (BR)** and **"}],"role":"model"},{"parts":[{"text":"Artificial Intelligence (AI)** is currently one of the most fertile areas of computer science research. While classical AI sought \"perfect"}],"role":"model"},{"parts":[{"text":"\" logic, modern AI research increasingly views intelligence through the lens of **Resource-Rationality**: the ability to make the best"}],"role":"model"},{"parts":[{"text":" possible decision given finite computational cycles, memory, and energy.\n\nThe following is a synthesis of the latest literature and research directions"}],"role":"model"},{"parts":[{"text":" regarding bounded rationality in AI.\n\n---\n\n### 1. Resource-Rational Analysis (The \"Optimal\" Bound)\nA"}],"role":"model"},{"parts":[{"text":" significant trend in AI literature, led by researchers like **Falk Lieder and Thomas Griffiths**, shifts the focus from \""}],"role":"model"},{"parts":[{"text":"Human vs. AI\" to **Resource-Rationality**. \n*   **The Concept:** Instead of viewing cognitive"}],"role":"model"},{"parts":[{"text":" biases as \"errors,\" this research models them as optimal strategies for a system with limited \"FLOPs\" (floating-point operations"}],"role":"model"},{"parts":[{"text":"). \n*   **AI Application:** Researchers are using these models to build \"Metacognitive AI\" that can"}],"role":"model"},{"parts":[{"text":" decide *how* to think before it actually thinks. This involves the AI calculating the \"Value of Computation\"—deciding whether to"}],"role":"model"},{"parts":[{"text":" spend more time processing a complex prompt or to give a \"good enough\" (satisficing) answer immediately.\n\n###"}],"role":"model"},{"parts":[{"text":" 2. \"System 2\" Reasoning in Large Language Models (LLMs)\nThe release of models like **OpenAI"}],"role":"model"},{"parts":[{"text":"’s o1** and research into **Chain-of-Thought (CoT)** prompting represents a practical implementation of bounded rationality."}],"role":"model"},{"parts":[{"text":"\n*   **Search vs. Inference:** Classical LLMs are \"System 1\" (fast, intuitive, but"}],"role":"model"},{"parts":[{"text":" prone to error). Current research focuses on \"Inference-time compute,\" where the model is given a \"budget\" to"}],"role":"model"},{"parts":[{"text":" perform a tree-search or self-reflection before answering. \n*   **The Literature:** Recent papers explore the"}],"role":"model"},{"parts":[{"text":" **\"Compute-Optimal Scaling Laws,\"** arguing that for complex tasks, the \"bounds\" of the model are defined"}],"role":"model"},{"parts":[{"text":" more by how much time it is allowed to \"reason\" at inference time than by how many parameters it has.\n\n### "}],"role":"model"},{"parts":[{"text":"3. Bounded Optimality and Algorithmic BR\n**Stuart Russell** and others have long advocated for **\""}],"role":"model"},{"parts":[{"text":"Bounded Optimality\"**—a framework where an agent is considered rational if its *program* is the best one that can run on"}],"role":"model"},{"parts":[{"text":" its specific hardware.\n*   **Hardware-Aware AI:** Latest research in \"TinyML\" and \"Edge AI\" focuses"}],"role":"model"},{"parts":[{"text":" on how to compress models (via pruning and quantization) so they remain \"rational\" despite severe memory bounds. \n*"}],"role":"model"},{"parts":[{"text":"   **Satisficing in Search:** In robotics and pathfinding, research is moving away from A* (which seeks the absolute shortest"}],"role":"model"},{"parts":[{"text":" path) toward **Anytime Algorithms**. These algorithms provide a \"satisfactory\" path instantly and improve it only if time"}],"role":"model"},{"parts":[{"text":" allows, mirroring Herbert Simon’s original definition of satisficing.\n\n### 4. Multi-Agent Systems and \""}],"role":"model"},{"parts":[{"text":"Theory of Mind\"\nIn environments where multiple AI agents interact (like autonomous driving or high-frequency trading), agents must account"}],"role":"model"},{"parts":[{"text":" for the fact that *other* agents are also boundedly rational.\n*   **Recursive Modeling:** Research into **\""}],"role":"model"},{"parts":[{"text":"Level-k Reasoning\"** in AI involves an agent thinking: *\"I think that you think that I am going to do"}],"role":"model"},{"parts":[{"text":" X.\"* Because this recursion is computationally expensive, agents must \"bound\" their depth of strategic thinking.\n*   **In"}],"role":"model"},{"parts":[{"text":"complete Information:** Recent literature in **Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning (MARL)** focuses on \"Robust Satisficing,\" where agents"}],"role":"model"},{"parts":[{"text":" seek strategies that work across a wide range of possible \"irrational\" behaviors from other agents.\n\n### 5. AI"}],"role":"model"},{"parts":[{"text":" Hallucinations as Bounded Failures\nNew perspectives in AI safety and ethics suggest that **hallucinations** are a"}],"role":"model"},{"parts":[{"text":" byproduct of bounded rationality.\n*   **The Argument:** An LLM has a \"context window\" (a limit"}],"role":"model"},{"parts":[{"text":" on how much it can remember at once). Hallucinations often occur when the model \"satisfices\" by filling in gaps"}],"role":"model"},{"parts":[{"text":" in its memory with statistically probable—but factually wrong—information. \n*   **Mitigation Research:** Literature"}],"role":"model"},{"parts":[{"text":" on **Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)** is essentially an attempt to expand the \"informational bounds\" of"}],"role":"model"},{"parts":[{"text":" an AI by giving it an external \"hard drive\" of facts to consult, reducing the need for internal (and fall"}],"role":"model"},{"parts":[{"text":"ible) computation.\n\n### 6. Thermodynamic AI and Energy Bounds\nClosely related to the \"Landauer-Wolpert"}],"role":"model"},{"parts":[{"text":" principle\" mentioned in general research, AI literature is now looking at **Energy-Delay Cost**.\n*   **Neuromorphic Computing"}],"role":"model"},{"parts":[{"text":":** Research into \"brain-like\" chips (like Intel's Loihi) aims to implement bounded rationality at the hardware"}],"role":"model"},{"parts":[{"text":" level. The goal is to create AI that operates on milliwatts of power, accepting \"good enough\" precision in"}],"role":"model"},{"parts":[{"text":" exchange for extreme energy efficiency, just as the human brain does.\n\n---\n\n### Key Scholars and Recent Papers to Follow"}],"role":"model"},{"parts":[{"text":":\n*   **Falk Lieder & Tom Griffiths:** For *Resource-Rational Analysis* and the link between human"}],"role":"model"},{"parts":[{"text":" cognition and AI.\n*   **Stuart Russell:** For *Bounded Optimality* and AI safety.\n*   **"}],"role":"model"},{"parts":[{"text":"Noam Brown (OpenAI/Meta):** For research on *Inference-time compute* and strategic reasoning ("}],"role":"model"},{"parts":[{"text":"e.g., Libratus and Cicero).\n*   **Yoshua Bengio:** Recent work on **\"System 2 Deep"}],"role":"model"},{"parts":[{"text":" Learning\"** which attempts to move AI beyond pattern recognition into deliberate, bounded reasoning.\n\n### Summary of the Pivot\nIn"}],"role":"model"},{"parts":[{"text":" AI, **Bounded Rationality is no longer seen as a limitation to be overcome, but as a design requirement.** The \""}],"role":"model"},{"parts":[{"text":"latest\" research has shifted from trying to build an \"all-knowing\" AI to building a **\"Compute-Efficient\""}],"role":"model"},{"parts":[{"text":" AI** that knows when to stop thinking and act."}],"role":"model"},{"parts":[{"text":"","thoughtSignature":""}],"role":"model"}]
